_meta.title: stage0 PBV list-growth memory leak — root cause + fix
_meta.track: anima extreme roadmap C3 (24h CLM stability)

root_cause.summary: stage0 interpreter implements arr[i]=v (Index assignment) by REBUILDING the entire host array via N successive .push calls into a fresh new_items list, then writes new_items into array_store[arr.int_val]. The OLD slot's items buffer is orphaned (never freed). Per-IndexAssign cost: ~2N HexaVal slots through realloc growth. Inside an inner loop W[i]=W[i]-lr*G[i] (N=2048), one outer step performs N IndexAssigns, each leaking an N-slot buffer → O(N²) RSS per step → OOM by step 250-500.
root_cause.mechanism: rebuild-via-push instead of in-place hexa_array_set. The host C runtime hexa_array_set already mutates in place (runtime.c:854), and the interpreter has a refcount + COW machinery (array_refcounts, array_push_inplace, array_decref) that works correctly for .push — but Index assignment was never updated to use it. The rebuild loop is purely vestigial.
root_cause.memory_math: isolate C: 50 outer steps × 2048 inner IndexAssigns = 102,400 IndexAssigns × ~32KB orphaned per call ≈ 3.2 GB orphan + 2-3 GB working set = ~5.9 GB (matches measurement)

fix_location.fix_pattern: let items = get_array(arr); items[idx.int_val] = val; array_store[arr.int_val] = items
fix_location.diff_summary: Replaced ~16-line rebuild-via-push loop with 3-line in-place mutation at all three sites. The host C runtime hexa_array_set is O(1) in place; PBV semantics still enforced at the array_store slot level via the existing array_refcounts mechanism.

commits.note: fix is uncommitted in /Users/ghost/Dev/hexa-lang/self/hexa_full.hexa (other uncommitted edits preexist there); commit responsibility belongs to hexa-lang session per c_track_board.json conventions

regression_test.note: combined RSS is the additive working set of all three back-to-back tests; individual tests peak much lower.

scaling_table_post_fix_pbv_leak_repro_sg.STEPS=500: 305 MB (run in test_soak_500 inside fn — fn-scope reclaim helps)

residual_findings.secondary_leak: approx 1.1 KB per while-loop iteration in the pure arithmetic case (no arrays, no IndexAssign). 100 outer × 2048 inner = 204,800 iters → 235 MB. STEPS=1000 of just-loop hits 2.3 GB.
residual_findings.secondary_leak_root_cause_hypothesis: WhileStmt env_push_scope/env_pop_scope per iteration plus val_float allocations not fully reclaimed by arena pop. Not in IndexAssign — verifiable by running /tmp/just_loop.hexa which has no arrays.
residual_findings.secondary_leak_severity: moderate — does NOT block C3's primary use case (pbv_leak_repro) which now runs at 124MB/50steps and 305MB/500steps. At realistic CLM step rate of ~1 outer step/s, residual 2 MB/step gives ~9 hours sustainable on 64 GB host RAM. Multi-day soaks would still need the secondary fix.
residual_findings.secondary_leak_recommendation: raise as a new C-track item (suggest C3-followup or new C12 'stage0 inner-loop allocation reclamation') with hexa_lang_session ownership.
